string.text:
The CAR-15 is a family of M16-based firearms marketed by Colt in the 1960s and 1970s, which are most commonly associated with compact, carbine-length versions like the Colt Commando. These firearms were designed for maneuverability, especially for use by special operations forces and aircrews during the Vietnam War. Today, "CAR-15" is often used as a generic term for carbine-length M16/AR-15 variants, with modern versions often incorporating updated components while retaining a classic profile.
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]ATTACHMENTS:
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]REFLEX SIGHTS:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] Aimpoint 2000
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]SCOPES:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] ACOG, ACOG 2x, Carry Handle Scope
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]SILENCER:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] NATO Suppressor

A modern take on the classic CAR-15, the Administrator was built out by an ex merc who wanted the classic profile of the CAR-15 carry handle, with all the capability of any modern day M4 build. Form over function baby.
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]ATTACHMENTS:
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]OPTIC:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] Aimpoint T2 with G33 Magnifier
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]SILENCER:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] NATO Suppressor

"If you know, you know. Ya Ya"
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]ATTACHMENTS:
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]REFLEX SIGHTS:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] Aimpoint Pro 
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]SILENCER:
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] Nato Suppressor
